rows.definition: "cache" is the bare umbrella word fak uses for several distinct reuse mechanisms at once: the in-kernel KV tensor store, the page-table virtualization (vCache), the provider prompt-cache fak only relays, the cachemeta metadata sidecar, and the cache-value P&L roll-up.
rows.distinction: It is the un-qualified family word that resolves to nothing on its own; every specific meaning (KV cache vs vCache vs cachemeta vs provider prompt-cache vs cachevalue P&L) is a different concept the more specific rows draw the line for.

rows.definition: The cachevalue stack (`fak cachevalue`, internal/cachevalueledger, internal/cachevaluereport, internal/cachevaluepost) is epic #1301's cache-effectiveness profit-and-loss roll-up: a durable append-only ledger of per-session reuse observations, folded into a by-week/by-session_type trend report, then posted as one Slack "cache-value" channel card configured by the FAK_CACHEVALUE_TOKEN/FAK_CACHEVALUE_CHANNEL resolution order.
rows.distinction: It is the off-path P&L accounting and reporting of whether the cache method is paying off over time, not the provider prompt-cache it measures nor the live KV cache that produces the reuse it tallies.

rows.definition: vcacheTurns is the gateway's bounded in-memory window of per-turn OBSERVED provider-cache counters (filled by metrics.observeVCacheTurn on every streamed passthrough turn, copied out via Server.VCacheTurnsSnapshot), which internal/vcachesnapshot persists to a JSONL file so a separate `fak vcache score`/observe process and the fak_vcache_* metric family can read the realized cache window a finished session saw.
rows.distinction: It is the read-only OBSERVED provider-cache telemetry window the gateway records and snapshots, not the cachevalue P&L roll-up that scores ledgered reuse nor the in-kernel KV cache that authors fak's own reuse.

rows.definition: ProviderCacheReadTokens (from the fak_gateway_inference_cached_prompt_tokens_total counter), ProviderCacheNetSavings, and the serving-engine PrefixCacheHitRate/cache_hit_rate ratios are the OBSERVED provider-and-engine cache numbers fak relays verbatim — the upstream provider's cache_read and the self-hosted engine's prefix hit rate, never numbers fak itself authored.
rows.distinction: Every number here is OBSERVED provider/engine telemetry relayed by fak, not the WITNESSED in-kernel KV-prefix reuse fak controls nor the price coefficient (CacheReadMultiplier) applied to value it.

rows.definition: lmcache references the external LMCache KV-cache layer (GPU->DRAM->disk->Redis cross-query non-prefix reuse) that fak positions against and governs without moving KV bytes, while MLCACHE1..MLCACHE6 are the plan/phase rung labels of fak's own docs/serving/multilevel-default-cache-epic.md multilevel-default-cache epic that lands the DRAM/disk/CXL pressure-aware placement those external systems motivate.
rows.distinction: These are positioning/study and epic-rung reference tokens, not the live in-kernel KV cache nor the cachemeta metadata plane through which external KV-transport systems report their events to fak.

rows.definition: engine.CacheEventRecorder (built by NewCacheEventRecorder) is the live serving-engine cache-event seam: the routing/offload path calls Record for each KV residency transition, which normalizes it into the shared cachemeta entry stream, derives the typed verdict, folds fak_engine_cache_* metrics, and returns a CacheEventResult.
rows.distinction: It is the engine-side recorder that normalizes residency transitions into the metric stream, not the cachemeta.Entry shape it emits nor the in-kernel vDSO CacheEventSink that observes tier-2 lifecycle events.

rows.definition: model.KVCacheToPaged snapshots a dense softmax KVCache into a fresh 3-plane paged sequence (the P/D-disaggregation and preemption swap path), and PagedKV.ToKVCache is its inverse that reconstructs a live contiguous *KVCache from a paged sequence's K/V pages.
rows.distinction: These are the dense-to-paged and paged-to-dense converters for moving KV between contiguous and page-table layouts, not the dense KVCache itself nor the page-table virtualization concept (vCache).

rows.definition: gateway.CachePricing is a model's base per-million-token input/output price whose CostUSD prices one turn's CacheUsage (uncached input at 1.0x, cache read at 0.1x, the provider's cache-create write at its TTL multiplier, output at the output rate), where CacheUsage is the four-axis token accounting (input/cacheRead/cacheCreate/output) the Anthropic usage block reports.
rows.distinction: It is the dollar cost model over a turn's billable token axes, not the provider prompt-cache it prices nor the cachevalue P&L roll-up that aggregates priced savings over time.

rows.definition: internal/cachewitness reads a live fak gateway's /metrics and folds the cache numbers into ONE provenance-labeled Record, splitting the WITNESSED in-kernel KV-prefix reuse fak controls from the OBSERVED provider cache_read it only relays, so a reader can never mistake fak's own reuse for the provider's.
rows.distinction: It is the provenance-labeled evidence fold over /metrics that keeps WITNESSED and OBSERVED cache numbers in separate fields, not the cachevalue ledger that persists ledgered observations nor the provider cache it observes.

rows.definition: agent.CompactReasonCachedSpan ("cached_span") is the conservative compaction outcome reason returned when a candidate history drop would delete cache_control-marked content, so the agent refuses to shed a span the provider prompt-cache is keyed on.
rows.distinction: It is the refuse-to-compact reason protecting a provider cache_control breakpoint span, not the provider cache itself nor the in-kernel KV span an evictor would reclaim.

rows.definition: In contextq, cacheability is the property that the same CapRef must resolve to the same Capability across a body, so a capability reference is stable enough to be reused/cached rather than re-resolved.
rows.distinction: It is the resolution-stability property of capability references in contextq, not any actual cache store nor the cachemeta metadata sidecar.

rows.definition: worktype.CacheOptimization ("cache-optimization") is the ongoing agent-memory-and-reuse program work class the epic classifier assigns to cache epics (e.g. #1301/#1351/#1217), labeling them as a continuous program rather than a discrete one-off.
rows.distinction: It is a work-classification label for cache-program epics, not any runtime cache nor the cachevalue P&L roll-up that one such epic produces.

rows.definition: DefaultCacheSize is the baseline LRU capacity integer literal the RSI loop tunes (internal/rsiloop and internal/vdso each declare one): the tier-2/vDSO cache slot count kpiprobe reports a hit-rate for and the worktree harness rewrites to measure a candidate.
rows.distinction: It is the LRU slot-count tunable knob the RSI loop sweeps, not the KV tensor store it bounds nor the cachevalue ledger that measures realized reuse.

rows.definition: cachePath is the `fak learningdebt --cache` flag value, the path to the learning-debt dispatch seen-cache JSON (default <workspace>/.fak/learning-debt-dispatch/seen.json) that records already-dispatched items.
rows.distinction: It is a CLI path flag for the learning-debt seen-cache file, not a KV or provider cache nor the cachevalue ledger path.

rows.definition: compactcohere.DefaultProviderCacheTTL (5 minutes) is the idle-expiry the harness-coherence coordinator assumes for the provider's prompt cache, used to decide when a provider cache entry has aged out between turns.
rows.distinction: It is the assumed idle TTL of the external provider prompt-cache, not the in-kernel KV cache's own lifetime nor the CacheReadMultiplier price coefficient.

rows.definition: contextq.NewViewCache returns an empty, concurrency-safe ViewCache that backs a context request's resolved-view reuse across recall/skill-memory/workflow-memory paths.
rows.distinction: It is the constructor for contextq's ViewCache (resolved context-view reuse), not the in-kernel KV cache nor the page-table vCache.

rows.definition: agent.PlaceAnthropicCacheBreakpoint rewrites a raw Anthropic request to place the cache_control breakpoint marker on the prompt prefix so the provider caches that span, returning a BreakpointOutcome describing what it did.
rows.distinction: It is the request rewriter that sets the provider cache_control breakpoint, not the cache_control marker concept itself nor rawHasCacheControl which only detects an existing one.
